findings.description: The prompt_packet field uses a machine-local /Users/... path, so another operator or control plane cannot resolve the measured prompt even though the hash is present.
The packet marks itself deployable and primary-reviewer capable even though g3_signed is false, so it bypasses the human G3 launch gate.
The evidence section leaves run_dir/report/lineage/pareto/trials empty, so a reviewer cannot trace the pinned composition back to measured run records.
The contract leaves trace_destination as TBD and trace_artifact empty, so launch review has neither a live sink nor an explicit JSONL-only waiver.
The contract allows direct GitHub PR comments and exposes GITHUB_TOKEN while g4_signed is false, expanding write authority before the human G4 gate.
The prompt_packet field uses an absolute machine-local path (/Users/phaedrus/...), binding the contract to a single workstation and preventing execution on other nodes.
g3_signed is false but deployable and primary_reviewer_allowed are both true, so an unsigned packet asserts deployment and primary-reviewer privileges without satisfying the required signing gate.
All evidence pointers (run_dir, report, lineage, pareto, trials) are empty strings, leaving the contract without the required audit and reproducibility artifacts.
The prompt_packet references a timestamped run directory path, which is a non-portable import reference that may fail to resolve outside the original workspace.
The contract sets g3_signed to false, so it represents an unsigned deployment that has not cleared the required G3 approval gate.
g3_signed is false, so the deployment is unsigned and cannot proceed through the approval gate.
trace_destination is set to the placeholder 'TBD' rather than a live trace sink, leaving no runtime telemetry.
trace_artifact is empty and no JSONL-only waiver is present, so there is no offline trace evidence for review.
The contract grants direct write authority to post GitHub PR comments while G4 approval remains unsigned, allowing production side effects before the required gate is satisfied.
